channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: US officials are bound for Europe for top-level talks on Ukraine
item.description: President Donald Trump’s national security adviser said Sunday that top administration officials will meet with European officials this week about how to end the war in Ukraine, nearly three years after Russia launched an all-out invasion.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says he is serious about Canada becoming 51st state in Super Bowl interview
item.description: President Donald Trump said he is serious about wanting Canada to become the 51st state in an interview that aired Sunday during the Super Bowl preshow.
item.dc:creator: Jill Colvin, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Richard Gere calls Trump a 'bully and a thug' at awards show
item.description: Hollywood star Richard Gere called President Trump a “bully” and a “thug” in his acceptance speech at the Academy of Cinematographic Arts and Sciences of Spain's Goya Awards on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: UN humanitarian chief says Gaza ceasefire has averted famine but any truce collapse brings danger
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Famine has been mostly averted in Gaza as a surge of aid enters the territory during a fragile ceasefire, the United Nations humanitarian chief said Sunday. But he warned the threat could return quickly if the truce collapses.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Parents of American murdered by Hamas make 'plea' to Trump after latest hostage release
item.description: The parents of murdered Israeli-American hostage Hersh Goldberg-Polin pleaded with President Donald Trump to get the remaining 76 hostages home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dalai Lama's elder brother, who led several rounds of talks with China, dies at 97
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — The elder brother of the Dalai Lama and former chairman of the Tibetan government-in-exile in India, Gyalo Thondup, who led several rounds of talks with China and worked with foreign governments for the Tibetan cause, has died. He was 97.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: 7.6 magnitude earthquake shakes the Caribbean, some islands urge residents to leave coastlines
item.description: A magnitude-7.6 earthquake shook the Caribbean Sea south of the Cayman Islands Saturday, according to the U.S. Geological Survey. Several islands and countries urged people near the coastline to move inland but authorities in most places later lifted the tsunami alerts.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ecuador votes for president, with the conservative incumbent and a leftist lawyer in the lead
item.description: Ecuadorians have voted in a presidential election that was shaping up to be a repeat of the 2023 race, when they chose a young, conservative millionaire over the leftist protégée of the country’s most influential president this century.
item.dc:creator: Regina Garcia Cano, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Kosovo votes for new parliament as foreign aid dwindles and talks with Serbia remain stalled
item.description: Kosovars are casting votes in a parliamentary election considered a key test for Prime Minister Albin Kurti as talks on normalizing ties with rival Serbia remain stalled.
item.dc:creator: Llazar Semini, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Sam Nujoma, Namibia's fiery freedom fighter and first president, dies aged 95
item.description: Sam Nujoma, the fiery freedom fighter who led Namibia to independence from apartheid South Africa in 1990 and served as its president for 15 years, has died.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Released Thai hostages return to Bangkok after being held for over a year in Gaza
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Five Thai workers released after being held hostage for over a year in Gaza arrived in Bangkok on Sunday. Sarusak Rumnao, 32, Watchara Sriaoun, 33, Sathian Suwannakham, 35, Pongsak Thaenna, 36, and Bannawat Saethao, 27, were freed on Jan. 30 as part of an exchange arrangement.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas releases 3 frail-looking Israeli hostages for Palestinian prisoners under Gaza ceasefire
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Hamas-led militants released three gaunt, frail-looking Israeli hostages and Israel freed nearly 200 Palestinian prisoners Saturday in the latest exchange of a ceasefire that has paused 16 months of war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mother, son die from food poisoning at Dominican Republic resort
item.description: A grieving family filed a lawsuit after a mother and son died from food poisoning while vacationing at a Dominican Republic resort.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, NewsNation

item.title: War-torn Lebanon forms its first government in over 2 years to end deadlock
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Lebanon’s new prime minister on Saturday formed the country’s first full-fledged government since 2022.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump halts aid to South Africa citing new land law
item.description: President Trump signed an executive order formally halting all aid to South Africa and urged his Cabinet to come up with a resettlement program for Afrikaners who he claims "are victims of unjust racial discrimination,” citing the country’s new land law intended to combat the racist apartheid era.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: State Department announces $7B arms sale to Israel after Congress blocked initial deal
item.description: The Trump administration announced a $7 billion arms sale to Israel, including munitions and missiles, just days after Congress blocked an initial deal.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: World's largest religious gathering in India underway as hundreds of millions visit 45-day festival
item.description: The largest religious gathering in the world is taking place in India from Jan. 13 to Feb. 26. It is a significant test for the country to showcase Hinduism and culture.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas frees 3 more hostages as part of ceasefire agreement with Israel
item.description: Hamas released three additional hostages on Saturday in exchange for dozens of Palestinian prisoners as part of the ceasefire deal the terror group reached with Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says he wants to negotiate about Ukraine. It's not clear if Putin really does
item.description: Nearly three years after President Vladimir Putin invaded Ukraine, his troops are making steady progress on the battlefield.
item.dc:creator: Emma Burrows, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A family fleeing violence in eastern Congo sees little hope as rebels grab more land
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — None of Zawadi Sifa's seven children have known life without fear and the constant threat of violence.
item.dc:creator: MONIKA PRONCZUK and JUSTIN KABUMBA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas names 3 more Israeli hostages to be freed as ceasefire deal stays on track
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Hamas identified three more Israeli hostages it plans to free as part of the fragile ceasefire agreement, a sign the deal was moving forward Friday even as U.S. and Israeli officials continued calls to relocate Gaza’s population after the war.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Hamas names next 3 Israeli hostages to be exchanged for Palestinian prisoners
item.description: Hamas named the next three Israeli hostages it plans to release this weekend as part of the Gaza ceasefire deal with Israel. It was a sign that the first phase of the truce was moving forward as planned -- even as many observers fear that U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump: We will have relations with North Korea
item.description: President Trump on Friday said the U.S. will have relations with North Korea, touting his personal relationship with Kim Jong Un, who rules the nuclear-armed country with an iron fist and has soldiers fighting on the front lines of Russia’s war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: A look at the hostages still held by Hamas in Gaza, by the numbers
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel and Hamas are in the midst of a six-week ceasefire during which Hamas is releasing dozens of the hostages captured in its Oct. 7, 2023, attack in exchange for hundreds of Palestinian prisoners.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump sanctions on ICC protects US military and government officials, former NSC staffer says
item.description: Amid the International Criminal Court’s issuance of arrest warrants for Israeli leaders for their role in waging a self-defense war against Hamas, President Donald Trump punished the global body, prompting strong reactions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A small plane loses altitude after takeoff and slams into a Sao Paulo avenue, killing 2 passengers
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — A small aircraft crashed on an avenue in Sao Paulo on Friday morning shortly after taking off from a nearby private airport, killing at least two of its passengers, the local firefighter corps said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Frozen US aid brings new challenges to groups hit by harsh crackdowns in Russia and Belarus
item.description: TALLINN, Estonia (AP) — Rights groups, activists and independent media in Russia and Belarus endured increased government scrutiny, repressive laws and even being outlawed and forced to operate from exile abroad. Many survived despite the difficulties.
item.dc:creator: DASHA LITVINOVA and YURAS KARMANAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Arab, Muslim Americans blast Trump's 'grotesque' Gaza proposal
item.description: Arab American and Muslim leaders are blasting President Trump’s proposal to take over Gaza, warning that it’s antithetical to their beliefs and amounts to ethnic cleansing.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, WGN 9

item.title: Zelenskyy wants nukes or NATO; Trump special envoy Kellogg says 'slim and none' chance
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Kyiv should get NATO membership or nukes to defend against Russia as Trump's special envoy heads to Europe for talks with allies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Arab nations decry Trump proposal for US to take over Gaza
item.description: Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas called for the United Nations to “protect the Palestinian people.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Jeffries amps up call for two-state solution after Trump push to seize Gaza
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) on Wednesday flatly rejected President Trump’s proposal for the United States to take over the Gaza Strip, suggesting it would undermine ongoing efforts to free Israeli hostages and bring peace to the volatile region.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, WGN 9

item.title: Earthquakes are rattling Greece's volcanic island of Santorini: What we know
item.description: Although Greece is no stranger to earthquakes, seismic activity of such frequency and intensity without having been preceded by a major quake is unusual, seismologists say.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US allies, adversaries reject Trump suggestion to 'take over' Gaza Strip
item.description: When the president was asked about possibly sending in U.S. troops to Gaza, he said America will “do what is necessary.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's suggestion the US 'take over' the Gaza Strip is rejected by allies and adversaries alike
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — President Donald Trump's proposal that the United States “take over” the Gaza Strip and permanently resettle its Palestinian residents was swiftly rejected and denounced on Wednesday by American allies and adversaries alike.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Saudi Arabia says it won't establish ties with Israel without creation of Palestinian state
item.description: Saudi Arabia said on Wednesday it would not establish ties with Israel without the creation of a Palestinian state.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Trump wants rare earth elements from Ukraine in exchange for military aid
item.description: “We're looking to do a deal with Ukraine, where they're going to secure what we're giving them with their rare earth and other things,” he told reporters.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WGN 9

item.title: EU warns Trump it would ‘respond firmly’ to tariffs
item.description: “The EU would respond firmly to any trading partner that unfairly or arbitrarily imposes tariffs on EU goods,” a spokesperson said. “There is a lot at stake.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation
